name: Pilot
summary: The ship's driver and primary defensive asset. The Pilot controls geometry — positioning, evasion, and pursuit. Every crew member's effectiveness depends on where the Pilot puts the ship. The Pilot is the only station that can establish or break [Locked On].

actions.name: Evasive Maneuvers
actions.description: Commit to defensive flying. The ship jukes, rolls, and changes vector to make targeting impossible. Taking Evasive Maneuvers breaks [Locked On] on any target — you abandoned pursuit to protect your ship.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. Incoming attacks have their Control die stepped down.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. Additionally, one ally station gains [Cover 1] from the ship's positioning.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Elusive] until start of Pilot's next turn. All incoming attacks are stepped down. Ship repositions 1 zone as a free movement.

actions.risk: On failure, the ship's vector is predictable — all attacks against the ship until the Pilot's next turn gain [Optimized].
actions.lockedOnInteraction: Taking Evasive Maneuvers automatically breaks [Locked On] on any target the Pilot was pursuing. You cannot evade and chase simultaneously.

actions.name: Attack Run
actions.description: Aggressive positioning — close distance, present firing solutions, and set up the Gunners. High reward, high risk.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] on their next attack this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their next attack this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship closes 1 zone. All Gunners gain [Optimized] and [Empowered]. The target ship presents an Opening to all Gunners.

actions.risk: On failure, the aggressive approach presents an Opening to the target ship's Gunners. The ship is [Exposed] until the Pilot's next turn.

actions.name: Pursue / Close
actions.description: Chase a fleeing target or close distance to engage. Roll Piloting (Reflex) vs the target Pilot's static Piloting defense. The only way to establish [Locked On].

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Close 1 zone toward the target. Maintain pursuit — the target cannot increase the gap this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Close 1 zone. The target gains [Locked On] — pursuit is locked. [Locked On] persists until the target Breaks Away at Masterful+, the locking Pilot takes an action that contradicts pursuit (Evasive Maneuvers, Terrain Run), or the target escapes the encounter.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Close 2 zones. The target gains [Locked On]. The locking ship's Gunners gain [Optimized] on attacks against the locked-on target.

actions.risk: On failure, the target increases the gap by 1 zone. Pursuit is not broken, but the target has the initiative.

actions.name: Break Away
actions.description: Disengage from combat. Increase distance, break pursuit, or set up a hyperspace jump. Roll Piloting (Reflex) vs pursuing Pilot's static Piloting defense.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Increase gap by 1 zone. If [Locked On], remain [Locked On]. Pursuit is not broken, but the ship gains breathing room.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Increase gap by 1 zone. [Locked On] is cleared. Pursuing ships lose contact — they cannot close distance or engage until the start of the next round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Increase gap by 2 zones. [Locked On] cleared. Pursuit is broken — the ship may jump to hyperspace if the Co-Pilot has plotted a course.

actions.risk: On failure, the ship cannot increase the gap. If [Locked On], remain [Locked On]. Pursuing ship's Gunners gain an Opening.

actions.name: Terrain Run
actions.description: Use the environment as a weapon — asteroid fields, debris clouds, atmospheric canyons, station infrastructure. Roll Piloting (Reflex) vs the pursuing Pilot's static Piloting defense. Your roll determines both your cover and how badly the terrain punishes anyone who follows. Taking Terrain Run breaks [Locked On] on any target — you veered into terrain, not continuing pursuit.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 1] from environmental obstruction until the Pilot's next turn.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 2]. Pursuing ships that follow into the terrain take Fleeting collision damage — the path you carved is yours alone.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Cover 2] and concealment. Pursuing ships that follow into the terrain take Masterful collision damage — the route you threaded was never meant for anyone else.

actions.risk: On failure, the Pilot clips the terrain — the ship takes Fleeting collision damage and loses [Cover] and concealment.
actions.lockedOnInteraction: Taking Terrain Run automatically breaks [Locked On] on any target the Pilot was pursuing. You cannot thread terrain and maintain pursuit simultaneously.

reactions.name: Evade
reactions.trigger: Ship is targeted by an attack
reactions.description: The ship's Dodge equivalent. The Pilot rolls Piloting + Handling to evade incoming fire. On success, the Power die result reduces the attacker's Effect Tier. On failure, no mitigation — the hit lands at full effect. On a Control result of 8+, the evasive maneuver generates an Opening for the ship's Gunners.
reactions.rule: Success: Power die result reduces attacker's Effect Tier. Failure: no mitigation. Control 8+: generates an Opening — any Gunner with an available Exploit pip may take a reactive attack against the attacker.
reactions.lockedOnInteraction: When the ship is [Locked On] by the attacker, the Evade Power die is [Weakened] — the pursuer knows your evasion patterns.

name: Gunner
summary: The ship's offensive firepower. The Gunner operates weapon mounts through the ship's Fire Control system, designates targets, and delivers the damage. Gunners don't control positioning — they execute within the geometry the Pilot established. Multiple Gunners can operate simultaneously if the ship has multiple mounts.

actions.name: Attack
actions.description: Fire a weapon mount at an enemy target. Uses the weapon's chassis damage table to determine damage dealt. Power die comes from the ship's Fire Control system.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Fleeting tier). Glancing hit — minimal structural impact.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Masterful tier). Solid hit — meaningful structural damage.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Legendary tier). Devastating hit — critical structural damage.

actions.risk: On failure, the shot misses. No damage dealt.

actions.name: Called Shot
actions.description: Target a specific system on the enemy ship. If the enemy crew chooses to impair a system to soak damage, they must impair the system you designated.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Fleeting tier). If the target crew impairs to soak, they must impair the designated system.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Masterful tier). Designated system must be impaired.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Weapon chassis damage (Legendary tier). Designated system must be impaired. Additionally, the designated system takes one extra step of impairment (Impaired becomes Debilitated, Debilitated becomes Offline).

actions.risk: On failure, the shot misses. The called targeting was too precise — no damage dealt.

actions.name: Suppressive Fire
actions.description: Saturate a zone with fire to deny movement and create pressure. No direct damage — tactical zone control.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target zone becomes suppressed — any ship that enters or exits the zone this round presents an Opening.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target zone is suppressed. All ships in the zone gain [Suppressed] — they present an Opening if they attempt to move.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target zone is suppressed. All ships in the zone gain [Suppressed] and [Disoriented] on their next action — the volume of fire disrupts targeting and coordination.

actions.risk: On failure, the suppression is ineffective. Ammunition is expended without tactical effect.

reactions.name: Exploit Opening
reactions.trigger: Pilot's Evade reaction generates an Opening (Control 8+)
reactions.description: When the Pilot's Evade generates an Opening, any Gunner with an available Exploit pip may take a reactive attack at -1 Effect Tier (default Exploit rules).
reactions.rule: Standard Exploit — reactive attack at -1 Effect Tier. Requires Exploit pip and a weapon mount that can target the attacker (arc and range must be valid).

gambits.name: Linked Fire
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Fire a second weapon mount in the same arc as part of the same attack action. The second mount resolves at the reduced tier. Both mounts spend ammunition/power.

gambits.name: Ion Pulse
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Instead of dealing hull damage, the attack impairs a system on the target ship. The tier determines impairment severity: Fleeting = Impaired, Masterful = Debilitated, Legendary = Offline. Only available on mounts with the Ion tag or when the Operator has applied [Jammed] to the target.

gambits.name: Overcharge
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. Step up the Fire Control Power die for this attack. After the attack resolves, the fired mount gains [Disabled] — it cannot fire until the Engineer spends an action to reset the weapon.

gambits.name: Concussive Blast
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. The attack detonates in a zone-wide blast. All ships in the target's zone are affected at the reduced tier. Only available on mounts with the Ordnance tag (missiles, torpedoes).

name: Operator
summary: The ship's electronic warfare specialist. The Operator scans, jams, and feeds targeting data to the crew. They are the crew's eyes and ears — and the enemy's nightmare on the sensor board. The Operator determines what the crew knows and what the enemy can do with their sensors.

actions.name: Scan
actions.description: The starship equivalent of Assess. Probe the target ship's systems, crew, and tactical disposition. Reveals information and can grant buffs to allies.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn one detail about the target: general ship class, approximate Integrity, or one system status (Impaired/Offline).

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn full system status of the target ship. Grant [Optimized] to one ally's next action against the scanned target.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Full system status plus crew disposition. Grant [Optimized] to all ally actions against the scanned target until end of round. Establish a target lock (required for torpedoes).

actions.risk: On failure, sensor data is inconclusive. The Operator learns nothing actionable. The target may detect the active scan.

actions.name: Jam
actions.description: Electronic attack — disrupt the target ship's sensors, communications, or targeting systems. Roll Tech (Wits) vs target Sensor resistance. Inflicts conditions on enemy systems.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target ship's Sensors are [Disoriented] until end of round — their Operator and Co-Pilot actions are stepped down.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target ship's Sensors are [Jammed] — their Operator actions automatically fail this round. Communications are disrupted.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target ship's Sensors are [Jammed] and all Gunner attacks from the target ship have their Control die stepped down — targeting data is corrupted.

actions.risk: On failure, the jamming signal is reflected — your own Sensors are [Disoriented] until end of round.

actions.name: Paint Target
actions.description: Feed precise targeting data to Gunners. The data is so precise that the Gunner's Control die is stepped up for their next attack.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: One Gunner gains [Optimized] on their next attack against the painted target.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: One Gunner gains [Optimized] on their next attack. Establishes a target lock (satisfies torpedo requirements).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: All Gunners gain [Optimized] on their next attack against the painted target. Target lock established. The targeting data is so precise it reveals one system vulnerability — the GM names one system that is already Impaired or damaged.

actions.risk: On failure, targeting data is unreliable. No buff granted. The active painting may alert the target to incoming ordnance.

reactions.name: Deploy Countermeasures
reactions.trigger: Ship is targeted by ordnance (missiles, torpedoes) or an electronic attack (Jam)
reactions.description: Release chaff, flares, or electronic decoys to disrupt incoming ordnance or jam attempts. Reduces the attacker's Effect Tier by the Operator's Power die result.
reactions.rule: Success: Power die result reduces attacker's Effect Tier against ordnance or electronic attacks. Failure: reduces by 1 Tier (minimum mitigation floor, like Endure). Countermeasure stores are finite — GM tracks depletion narratively.

gambits.name: Ghost Protocol
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a successful Jam, the target ship loses all target locks on your ship. Additionally, your ship gains concealment on sensors — the target cannot use guided ordnance against your ship until the concealment is broken (your Pilot enters the same zone, your ship attacks the target, or the Operator's Jam effect expires).

gambits.name: Slice Systems
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a successful Jam at Masterful+, instead of disrupting sensors, directly impair one system of the Operator's choice on the target ship. The intrusion is deep — the impairment lasts until the target's Engineer repairs it.

name: Engineer
summary: The ship's mechanic and damage control specialist. The Engineer keeps systems running, reinforces defenses, and reroutes power where it is needed most. They do not fire weapons — they make sure everything else works. The Engineer is what keeps a losing fight survivable.

actions.name: Reinforce Shields
actions.description: Proactively boost shield output before the next hit lands. Creates a damage buffer that absorbs incoming fire.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 1] — absorbs 1 point of incoming hull damage before it lands.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 2]. The shield reinforcement is clean — no power drain to other systems.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Ship gains [Buffered 3]. Additionally, the shields are so overcharged that the next attack against the ship has its Effect Tier reduced by 1 (shield flare disrupts targeting).

actions.risk: On failure, the shield harmonics destabilize. No buffer gained. Shields are [Disoriented] until the Engineer's next turn.

actions.name: Jury Rig
actions.description: Repair an impaired or offline system. The Power die is the damaged system's current die level — worse damage means a weaker Power die, making critical repairs harder. All repairs are permanent. Failure is a wasted action with no additional penalty.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Tactical bypass — the system's impairment is ignored for 1 round. The system operates at its pre-damage level until the end of the next round, then reverts. The damage is not repaired — the Engineer is routing around it temporarily.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Permanent repair — the system recovers 1 step of impairment. Debilitated becomes Impaired. Impaired becomes Operational. Offline becomes Debilitated.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Major repair — recover 2 steps of impairment on one system, OR recover 1 step on two different systems. The kind of repair that should take a dock crew two hours, done under fire in thirty seconds.

actions.risk: On failure, the repair attempt fails. The system remains in its current state. Wasted action — no additional penalty.

actions.name: Reroute Power
actions.description: Trade power between systems — step down one system to step up another. Tactical resource management under fire.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up another system's die by 1 size. Lasts 1 round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up another system's die by 1 size. Lasts until the end of the fight or until rerouted again.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Step down one system's die by 1 size. Step up the target system's die by 2 sizes. Lasts until the end of the fight. The power routing is so efficient it exceeds the system's rated output.

actions.risk: On failure, the power transfer is unstable. The source system steps down but the target system does not step up. The imbalance persists for 1 round.

name: Co-Pilot
summary: The crew's coordinator and force multiplier. The Co-Pilot does not fly the ship or fire the guns — they make everyone else better at their jobs. Navigation, tactical coordination, and crew support. The Co-Pilot is what separates a crew from a collection of individuals.

actions.name: Coordinate
actions.description: Direct the crew's efforts, share tactical data, and synchronize actions. The starship equivalent of the Command maneuver.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] to one crew member's next action this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] to one crew member and [Empowered] to a different crew member for their next actions this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Grant [Optimized] and [Empowered] to all crew members for their next actions this round. The coordination is seamless.

actions.risk: On failure, the coordination is muddled. No buffs granted. One crew member (GM's choice) has their next action [Disoriented] from conflicting instructions.

actions.name: Assist Pilot
actions.description: Directly support the Pilot's actions — managing secondary controls, calling out vectors, or taking the stick for repositioning.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] on their next action or Evade reaction this round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their next action or Evade reaction this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Pilot gains [Optimized] and [Empowered]. Additionally, the ship may reposition 1 zone as a free movement without requiring a Pilot action.

actions.risk: On failure, the assistance is counterproductive. The Pilot's next action suffers [Disoriented] from conflicting control inputs.

actions.name: Plot Course
actions.description: Calculate navigation solutions — hyperspace jumps, escape routes, or optimal approach vectors.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Rough hyperspace calculation — jump is possible but imprecise. The GM may impose a navigation complication on arrival.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Clean hyperspace calculation — jump is ready when the Pilot calls for it. No complications. Alternatively, identify one optimal escape route that grants +1 Tier to the next Break Away.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Precision jump — ready with tactical advantage. Alternatively, identify a shortcut route granting +2 Tier to the next Break Away and concealment during the approach.

actions.risk: On failure, calculation error. Jump not available this round. If the crew was counting on an escape route, the next Break Away is [Weakened].

gambits.name: Anticipate
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a Coordinate action, the buffed crew member's action cannot be [Disoriented] or [Weakened] by enemy abilities until end of round.

gambits.name: Calculated Withdrawal
gambits.rule: Reduce your Effect by 1 Tier. On a Plot Course action at Masterful+, additionally all crew members gain +1 Tier on defensive reactions until end of round. The crew knows the exit is ready — they fight with clarity.
